title: TROUBLE DIAGNOSIS

rows.cause:
Accelerator switch abnormality (when accelerator switch stays "ON")
• After engine starts, vehicle does not move. When the accelerator position switch operates, vehicle can start moving above engine rpm of 1,100 in "D" or "Ds" range because fail-safe mode is set.
rows.countermeasure:
• Check accelerator switch
• Check accelerator switch function
• Check harness to N-CVT control unit
• Replace accelerator switch

rows.cause:
Engine ground is faulty
• Engine speed fluctuates and is not smooth
rows.countermeasure: • Check for the ground terminal tightness.

rows.cause:
Connector contact is faulty
• Problem condition occurs intermittently or irregularly. This also occurs because of vehicle vibration.
Related connectors:
1. Inhibitor switch connectors
2. N-CVT control unit connectors
   ① Range signal
   ② Clutch circuit output
3. Brush holder connector in engine room
rows.countermeasure:
• Check insertion condition of connectors (Terminal open or removed)
• Check terminal crimping for disconnection
• Replace terminal

rows.cause: Improper inhibitor switch contact. (Dust or debris adheres to contact point or contact point operation is faulty)
rows.countermeasure:
• Check inhibitor switch
• Replace inhibitor switch

rows.cause:
Brush holder is faulty:
• Harness disconnection, faulty sealing or short circuit
• Brush distortion
• Soiled brush or slip ring (oil leak or water damage)
• Foreign matter in brush end, brush wear

• The brush holder itself is usually not damaged. In most cases, chips or particles (iron/aluminium) in the clutch housing short-circuit the slip ring or brush, or oil and water leaks cause incorrect connection.

• When there is a slip ring or brush short-circuit, this causes N-CVT control unit damage which may result in improper selector operation, cause the engine to stop in the drive range or idle incorrectly. However, there is an N-CVT protection function which disconnects the clutch when a clutch short-circuit is detected.
rows.countermeasure:
• Replace brush holder (except where there is a short circuit)
• Replace oil seal (oil leaks)
• Check or repair housing seal (when water has penetrated)
• Remove foreign matter from housing

rows.cause:
Insufficient clutch torque capacity, incorrect clutch operation
• Burn damage (powder loses magnetic characteristics)
• The clutch is exposed to water or oil
• Powder leak or short-circuit (The vehicle may not start to move)

• When the capacity insufficiency is slight, the vehicle may be driven even though the clutch may slip often. The condition will become more severe and the vehicle will not move at all eventually
rows.countermeasure:
• Perform the stall test to check for insufficient capacity
• Clutch replacement (insufficient capacity)
• Oil seal replacement (oil leak)

model_notes: N-CVT (Nissan Continuously Variable Transmission) specific. References D and Ds range selector positions.
